steps.title: Express the target as a linear combination
steps.content:
We don't need to find $x$ and $y$ individually—just the value of $3x - 5y$. Notice that $3x - 5y$ can be written as a linear combination of the given equations. If we set $a(x + 4y) + b(4x - y) = 3x - 5y$, expanding gives $(a + 4b)x + (4a - b)y = 3x - 5y$. Matching coefficients:
$$a + 4b = 3$$
$$4a - b = -5$$

steps.title: Solve for a and b
steps.content:
Multiply the first equation by 4: $4a + 16b = 12$. Subtract the second equation:
$$(4a + 16b) - (4a - b) = 12 - (-5)$$
$$17b = 17$$
$$b = 1$$
Substitute back: $a = 3 - 4(1) = -1$.

steps.title: Apply the linear combination to find the answer
